author: Claude
message:
Rebase from nightly

test(template,phone): add regression tests for issue #82 empty SIM placeholder

Extract the pure substitution loop out of Template.render into a JVM-static
substitute() helper so it can be exercised without an Android Context or
MMKV native init. Add TemplateTest covering the placeholder grammar and
pinning the user-visible failure mode from issue #82: when Phone.getSimDisplayName
returns "" (slot extra missing, or SecurityException caught upstream),
{{SIM}} collapses and the forwarded message renders as "[接收短信]" with no
SIM marker at all.

Also extend PhoneTest with the bug reporter's exact configuration —
dual-SIM China Mobile with and without hide_phone_number — and a case
where displayName is blank so formatSimDisplayName must fall back to the
operator name.

author: qwe7002
message:
Refactor job IDs and improve notification handling

- Introduced JobIds object to centralize job ID management for KeepAlive and ReSend jobs.
- Enhanced NotificationService to manage notification edits more effectively, including handling pending updates and persisting notification states.
- Added NotificationEditStore for managing notification-to-Telegram message mappings, ensuring updates edit existing messages instead of creating new ones.
- Implemented a new ChatSession data structure to replace the previous single-slot chat state management, allowing for concurrent sessions without data leakage.
- Improved Resend functionality with atomic access to the resend list to prevent concurrent modification issues.
- Updated DataMigrationManager to handle changes in data structure versioning and clean up deprecated keys.
- Added new constants for session management and notification editing.

author: Claude
message:
refactor(template,phone): address copilot review feedback on PR #83

- Template.render: cache the resource default so getStringByName is only
  resolved once, and let the Elvis fallback guard the actual nullable —
  MMKV decode — rather than re-do the lookup.
- Phone.getSimDisplayName: pass the SecurityException as the throwable
  argument to Log.w so the stack trace is preserved in field reports,
  instead of interpolating the (possibly null) e.message into the text.

author: Claude
message:
fix(phone): degrade to name-only when READ_PHONE_NUMBERS denied (#82)

After upgrading to v26.05, the runtime-revocable READ_PHONE_NUMBERS
permission can end up un-granted, so subscriptionManager.getPhoneNumber()
throws SecurityException on Android 13+. The exception was bubbling up to
SMSReceiver's outer catch, which silently set dualSim to "" and made the
{{SIM}} placeholder vanish entirely from the forwarded message — leaving
just "[接收短信]" as reported.

Catch the SecurityException at the source instead and pass phoneNumber=null
into formatSimDisplayName, which already produces a "name " form. This also
benefits CallReceiver, WAPReceiver and ChatService callers that never had an
outer catch and would have crashed on the same exception.

Add a PhoneTest regression that pins the contract: with phoneNumber=null
(the exact state after the SecurityException catch), the carrier name
must still surface so the SIM marker remains visible.
